name: Savon
designer: Léo Guibert, Fanny Hamelin, Loan Bottex, Marion Sendral
classification_notes: The specimen text and style suggest a classical text serif with upright roman, italic, and bold cuts, typical of transitional or humanist serif construction suited to extended reading.
summary: A three-style text serif produced in 2018 by four designers at E162, covering regular, italic and bold with 639 characters each.
description: Designed collaboratively by Léo Guibert, Fanny Hamelin, Loan Bottex and Marion Sendral, Savon is a text serif offered in three styles: regular, italic and bold. Each style carries 639 characters, suggesting solid Latin coverage for French-language setting. The typeface is available on request and accompanied by a printed specimen accessible via the E162 print subdomain.
foundry_blurb: Savon is a typeface designed at E162 by Léo Guibert, Fanny Hamelin, Loan Bottex and Marion Sendral in 2018. It is available in regular, italic and bold, each with 639 characters, and can be requested directly from the foundry. A physical specimen is available through the E162 print site.

name: Daewang
classification_notes: The typeface is presented as a display font by E162, with limited contextual detail but positioned as a bespoke or experimental design available on request, suggesting a display-oriented character.
summary: A single-weight display typeface by Rafael Ribas, released through the French micro-foundry E162 in 2017 and available on request.
description: Designed by Rafael Ribas and released in 2017, Daewang is offered through E162 as a bespoke typeface available on direct request rather than standard retail. The character set runs to 670 glyphs, suggesting reasonable Latin coverage for a single-weight design. Its availability via specimen print and direct contact places it firmly in the tradition of small French foundry releases aimed at considered, project-specific use.
foundry_blurb: E162 present Daewang as a typeface by Rafael Ribas, released in 2017 and available on request. A print specimen can be obtained via the E162 print site, and the font is distributed directly by the designer.

name: Héritier
classification_notes: Héritier has distinct Book and Display optical cuts alongside a Black weight, suggesting a traditionally structured serif with contrasting stroke modulation suited to both text and headline settings.
summary: A two-optical-size serif with Book and Display cuts, designed by Léo Guibert and Gabriel Vaury at E162 in 2017.
description: Designed by Léo Guibert and Gabriel Vaury, Héritier offers five styles across two optical registers: a Book cut for running text and a Display cut for larger settings, each available in roman and italic, plus a standalone Black weight. The 559-character set suggests reasonable language coverage for Western European Latin typography. Its origin in a French design school context gives it a considered, academic tone suited to editorial and cultural publishing.
foundry_blurb: E162 present Héritier as a typeface designed by Léo Guibert and Gabriel Vaury in 2017, available in Book, Book Italic, Display, Display Italic and Black, with a 559-character set. Specimens are available via print.e162.eu, and licences are obtained on request.

name: Tactic
classification_notes: Tactic presents as a clean, upright grotesque with a complementary italic, consistent with the rationalist sans-serif tradition common to French type design of the 2010s.
summary: A spare grotesque by Antoine Elsensohn, offered in Regular and Italic with a character set of 485 glyphs, available directly from the designer.
description: Designed by Antoine Elsensohn and released in 2018 under the French foundry E162, Tactic is a restrained grotesque available in Regular and Italic cuts. The character set covers 485 glyphs, suggesting reasonable Latin coverage for European languages. Specimens and licensing are handled directly by the designer on request, keeping distribution intentionally small-scale.
foundry_blurb: E162 presents Tactic as a typeface by Antoine Elsensohn, released in 2018, available in Regular and Italic. Typographies are available on request, with a print specimen accessible via print.e162.eu.

name: Utopia
classification_notes: The sample text and specimen imagery suggest a classical text serif with regularised proportions typical of transitional designs; the name Utopia and its single upright roman weight reinforce a restrained, text-oriented disposition.
summary: A single-weight text serif by Axel Pelletanche-Thévenart, released in 2016 through the small French foundry E162 and available on request.
description: Released in 2016, this roman serif exists as a single Regular weight with a character set of 135 glyphs. Designed by Axel Pelletanche-Thévenart and distributed through E162, it is available only by direct enquiry rather than through a conventional retail channel. The restrained scope suits careful editorial or typographic research contexts rather than broad commercial deployment.
foundry_blurb: E162 present Utopia as a typeface by Axel Pelletanche-Thévenart, released in 2016 and available on request by email. A specimen can be obtained via their print subdomain.

name: Héritier Stencil
classification_notes: A stencil typeface offered in Book and Display optical sizes with an italic variant, placing it firmly in the display category with a stencil subcategory.
summary: A stencil typeface by Léo Guibert and Gabriel Vaury, offered in Book and Display optical sizes with a Display Italic, covering 421 characters.
description: Designed by Léo Guibert and Gabriel Vaury at E162 and released in 2017, Héritier Stencil provides three subfamilies calibrated to specific size ranges: Book for 12–16pt settings, and Display and Display Italic for 18–30pt. The 421-character set gives reasonable coverage for French and extended Latin text. Available to licence on request directly from the foundry.
foundry_blurb: E162 present Héritier Stencil as a typeface by Léo Guibert and Gabriel Vaury, available in Book and Display optical sizes alongside a Display Italic, with specimens available via the foundry's print specimen site and licences obtainable on request.

name: Didier
classification_notes: The name and specimen text point clearly to a Didot-influenced design; the high contrast between thick and thin strokes and vertical stress characteristic of didone typefaces is implied by the French typographic tradition the foundry references.
summary: A single-weight didone designed by Axel Pelletanche-Thévenart in 2015, available on request from the French micro-foundry E162.
description: Released in 2015 by Axel Pelletanche-Thévenart under E162, Didier is a didone serif with a 281-character Latin character set. The typeface is available to licence on request directly from the designer, with a printed specimen available via a dedicated print subdomain. The family currently offers one standard weight with no italic or condensed cuts.
foundry_blurb: E162 presents Didier as a typeface designed by Axel Pelletanche-Thévenart in 2015, offered in a single standard style with 281 characters, and available on request. A physical specimen can be obtained through the foundry's print service.

name: Contractor Mono
classification_notes: A monospaced typeface explicitly labelled Mono, with a single upright style and no evident italic or width variants, designed for typographic use in both body and display contexts.
summary: A monospaced typeface by Loan Bottex and Léo Guibert, released in 2018 through the French independent foundry E162, available on request.
description: Designed by Loan Bottex and Léo Guibert and released in 2018, Contractor Mono is a monospaced typeface from the small French foundry E162. The family covers 162 characters and is distributed by request rather than through a conventional storefront. A printed specimen is available via a dedicated print site, reflecting the foundry's considered approach to distribution.
foundry_blurb: E162 present Contractor Mono as a typeface available on request, designed by Loan Bottex and Léo Guibert in 2018, with a printed specimen available through their dedicated print site.

name: Ventoux
classification_notes: The specimen text and single Light weight presentation suggest a refined humanist sans-serif design, with the name and French foundry context pointing toward a clean, text-oriented approach.
summary: A single-weight humanist sans-serif designed by Guillaume Letellier for French independent foundry E162, available on request.
description: Designed by Guillaume Letellier and released in 2017, Ventoux is offered in a single Light weight with a character set of 338 glyphs. The typeface is distributed on request through E162, a small French foundry, with a printed specimen available separately. Its restrained weight range and humanist construction suit long-form editorial text.
foundry_blurb: E162 present Ventoux as a typeface designed by Guillaume Letellier in 2017, available in a Light weight with 338 characters. It can be obtained on request, with a printed specimen available via the foundry's print subdomain.

name: Mach
classification_notes: The specimen and visual context suggest a clean, constructed sans-serif with geometric qualities, consistent with contemporary French type design practice.
summary: A single-weight geometric sans by Samuel Pin, available on request from the E162 foundry.
description: Designed by Samuel Pin in 2017 and released through the French foundry E162, Mach is offered in a single weight covering 767 characters. Distribution is handled directly via the designer, with a printed specimen available through a dedicated E162 print site. The narrow release scope and request-only availability suggest a considered, limited edition approach to distribution.
foundry_blurb: Mach is a typeface by Samuel Pin, released in 2017 under the E162 foundry. It is available on request by contacting the designer directly, and a physical specimen can be obtained via the E162 print site.

name: Caligula
classification_notes: Caligula is presented in three optical grades — Text, Display and Poster — suggesting a serif designed with optical size in mind, typical of a classical or transitional serif lineage adapted for contemporary use across scales.
summary: A three-grade serif by Alexandre Debelloir, designed in 2017 with distinct cuts for text, display and poster settings.
description: Designed by Alexandre Debelloir and released through the French foundry E162 in 2017, Caligula spans three optical grades — Text, Display and Poster — each tuned for its intended size range. The character set runs to 245 glyphs, covering standard Latin. Specimens and licences are available on request directly from the foundry.
foundry_blurb: E162 present Caligula as a typeface by Alexandre Debelloir, available in three grades — Text, Display and Poster — with specimens available via print.e162.eu and licences on request.

name: Garamond Grotesk
classification_notes: The name references Garamond as a historical serif source, but the typeface is presented as a grotesque sans-serif, suggesting a hybrid drawing that strips serif structures from classical Garamond forms.
summary: A grotesque sans drawn from Garamond's classical structures by Loan Bottex, available on request from the E162 foundry.
description: Released in 2017 by Loan Bottex under the E162 label, Garamond Grotesk takes the proportions and skeleton of Garamond and reinterprets them through a sans-serif grotesque lens. Currently available in a single Semi Bold weight with a character set of 441 glyphs, the typeface is distributed on request rather than through a conventional retail channel. The project sits within a broader inquiry into digital interfaces and the inherited conventions of print typography.
foundry_blurb: E162 present Garamond Grotesk as a typeface by Loan Bottex, released in 2017 and available on request. A specimen is available via print.e162.eu, with licensing enquiries directed to the designer directly.

name: Strike
classification_notes: Available only in a single Black weight, Strike is a high-contrast display typeface designed for headline use at large sizes, placing it firmly in the display category.
summary: A single-weight Black display typeface by Loan Bottex, released in 2017 and designed for large-scale typographic impact.
description: Designed by Loan Bottex and released in 2017, Strike exists solely in a Black weight, making it a typeface built for maximum typographic presence at headline sizes. The character set covers 812 glyphs, offering reasonable depth for a display cut. It is sold via Playtype and distributed through the E162 foundry.
foundry_blurb: Strike is a Black display typeface by Loan Bottex, released in 2017 through E162 and available via Playtype. A physical specimen can be obtained from print.e162.eu.

name: Navigator
classification_notes: The specimen and visual weight suggest a clean geometric sans-serif construction. The single Semi Bold weight and minimal character set point to a display-oriented geometric typeface.
summary: A single-weight geometric sans-serif by Antoine Elsensohn, released through French foundry E162 in 2016.
description: Designed by Antoine Elsensohn and available on request through E162, Navigator offers a single Semi Bold weight with a character set of 220 glyphs. The typeface is positioned for editorial and display use, with a printed specimen available via the foundry's print portal. Its limited weight range and restrained character set give it a focused, purpose-built character.
foundry_blurb: E162 present Navigator as a typeface by Antoine Elsensohn, released in 2016 and available on request. A printed specimen can be obtained via print.e162.eu, and the type is offered directly through the designer.

name: Attilio
classification_notes: The specimen text and general character suggest a traditional serif design suited to extended reading; the two-weight offering with Light and Bold variants points to a text-focused transitional or humanist serif classification.
summary: A two-weight text serif by Cécile Legnaghi, available via E162 on request with a 430-character set.
description: Designed by Cécile Legnaghi and released in 2015, Attilio offers Light and Bold weights across a 430-character Latin set. The typeface is distributed by E162 on request rather than through direct purchase, with specimens available via a dedicated print site. The two-weight structure suits sustained reading in editorial and publication contexts.
foundry_blurb: E162 presents Attilio, designed by Cécile Legnaghi in 2015, available in Light and Bold weights with 430 characters. Typographies are available on request, and printed specimens can be obtained through the E162 print site.

name: Damocles
classification_notes: The specimen imagery and the single-weight, text-focused design suggest a classical serif with transitional characteristics, suitable for book and editorial settings.
summary: A single-weight text serif by Antoine Elsensohn, released in 2017 and available on request from E162.
description: Designed by Antoine Elsensohn and published through the French foundry E162, Damocles is a text serif issued in a single Regular weight covering 223 characters. The specimen, available in print form via E162's print portal, is presented with body copy exploring ideas around alphabetical ordering and language. It is distributed on request rather than through a standard retail channel.
foundry_blurb: E162 present Damocles as a typeface by Antoine Elsensohn, designed in 2017 and available on request. A print specimen can be obtained through their dedicated specimen site, and enquiries are directed to the designer directly.

name: Royal
classification_notes: The two extreme weights, Light and Black, suggest a display-oriented serif designed for contrast and typographic impact rather than extended text use.
summary: A two-weight display serif by My-Lan Thuong, offering only a Light and a Black for maximum typographic contrast.
description: Designed by My-Lan Thuong and released in 2018 under E162, Royal comes in just two weights: Light and Black, making it a tool for high-contrast display settings rather than continuous text. The character set covers 188 glyphs in Latin script. Specimens are available via the E162 print site, with fonts distributed on request.
foundry_blurb: Royal is a typeface by My-Lan Thuong, released in 2018 and available in Light and Black weights. Fonts are available on request, and printed specimens can be obtained through the E162 print portal.

name: Blood
classification_notes: Blood appears as an all-capitals display typeface with two stylistic variants, suited to titling and headline use rather than extended text setting. Its uppercase-only specimen presentation and distinct stylistic alternates place it firmly in the display category.
summary: An all-capitals display typeface by Marc Sacier, released in two stylistic variants through the small French foundry E162.
description: Designed by Marc Sacier in 2017 and available through E162, Blood comes in two subfamilies — South 101 and South 102 — each offering a distinct stylistic register within the same all-capitals display framework. The character set runs to 341 glyphs and the typeface is available on request directly from the foundry. Its uppercase-only presentation and editorial specimen copy suggest a primary use in titling, poster and screen display contexts.
foundry_blurb: E162 offer Blood as a display typeface by Marc Sacier, presented in two variants — South 101 and South 102 — with 341 characters and available on request via email or through their print specimen site.

name: Sultan
classification_notes: The specimen text and formal structure suggest a text-oriented serif design; with limited detail available, a transitional serif classification fits the visible letterform rhythm and intended editorial usage.
summary: A single-weight text serif by Cécile Legnaghi, available on request from E162's French type foundry.
description: Designed by Cécile Legnaghi and released in 2017, Sultan is a text serif offered in a single Regular weight covering 148 characters. Available by request rather than through a standard retail channel, it sits within E162's small-batch approach to type publishing. The specimen positions it for editorial and long-form reading contexts.
foundry_blurb: E162 presents Sultan as a typeface by Cécile Legnaghi, released in 2017 and available on request. A printed specimen can be obtained via the foundry's print site, and enquiries are directed to the designer directly.

name: Golgotha
classification_notes: The page presents two subfamily styles, Atlas and Axis, suggesting a structured serif family; the sample text and specimen context point to a text-oriented transitional or classical serif design.
summary: A serif family by Rafael Ribas released in 2016, comprising two named styles, Atlas and Axis, with a character set of 305 glyphs.
description: Designed by Rafael Ribas and published through E162, Golgotha is structured around two distinct subfamilies, Atlas and Axis, intended for text and titling use respectively. The family covers 305 characters and is available on request directly from the designer. Specimen materials are available via the E162 print portal.
foundry_blurb: E162 present Golgotha as a typeface by Rafael Ribas, released in 2016 and offered in two variants, Atlas and Axis. The family is available on request, with physical specimens obtainable through the E162 print site.

name: Sonar
classification_notes: Sonar presents as a clean, upright sans-serif with a humanist character, offered in five weights that suggest optical refinement and readability-focused design rather than geometric or grotesque traditions.
summary: A five-weight humanist sans-serif designed by Alexandre Debelloir in 2018, available through the French studio E162 on request.
description: Designed by Alexandre Debelloir and released in 2018 under the E162 foundry, Sonar covers 269 characters across five weights from Light to Bold. The character set is modest and Latin-only, suited to French-language editorial and text work. It is distributed on request rather than through a conventional retail channel.
foundry_blurb: E162 present Sonar as a typeface by Alexandre Debelloir, released in 2018, available in five weights and obtainable by contacting the designer directly or via the E162 specimen print site.

name: Gebaude Grotesk
classification_notes: Named 'Grotesk' and presented in a single upright weight with no italics or condensed cuts, consistent with a traditional grotesque sans-serif design.
summary: A single-weight grotesque released in 2015 by French designer Axel Pelletanche-Thévenart under the E162 foundry, available on request.
description: A modest grotesque sans-serif with a character set of 208 glyphs, issued in one Regular weight without italic or condensed companions. Released under the small French foundry E162, the typeface is available only on request via the designer's contact, suggesting a limited or bespoke distribution model. Suited to text and titling work where a clean, unadorned grotesque is required.
foundry_blurb: E162 present Gebaude Grotesk as a typeface designed by Axel Pelletanche-Thévenart in 2015, available on request, with a specimen accessible via their print subdomain.

name: Infernus
classification_notes: The name, the single weight offering, and the gothic/infernal aesthetic implied by the branding suggest a display-oriented typeface with expressive or decorative intent rather than text utility.
summary: A single-weight display typeface by Marc Sacier, available by request from the French foundry E162.
description: Released in 2016 by Marc Sacier under the French foundry E162, Infernus is offered in a single Medium weight covering 343 characters. Its availability only on request and the accompanying specimen site suggest a limited, considered release aimed at editorial or expressive applications. The foundry's French-language presentation and niche distribution model place it firmly within independent European type culture.
foundry_blurb: E162 presents Infernus as a typeface by Marc Sacier, released in 2016 and available only on request. A physical specimen can be obtained via the foundry's print site, and the single Medium weight covers 343 characters.

name: BigPoppa
classification_notes: BigPoppa is offered in two styles, Fat and Fat Italic, with a name and weight naming convention typical of fat face display types designed for large-scale headline use.
summary: A two-style fat face display type by Victor Fonseca, offering Fat and Fat Italic cuts across a 262-character set.
description: Designed by Victor Fonseca and released in 2015 under the French foundry E162, BigPoppa is a fat face display type offered in Fat and Fat Italic. With 262 characters, it covers a working Latin character set suited to headline and poster use. Available on request directly from the foundry.
foundry_blurb: E162 present BigPoppa as a display typeface by Victor Fonseca, available in Fat and Fat Italic. Specimens can be obtained via print.e162.eu, and the typeface is available on request.

name: IJBurg
classification_notes: Based on the specimen rendering, IJBurg presents as a French grotesque-style sans-serif with a neutral, utilitarian character suited to both text and display use.
summary: A French grotesque sans-serif by Quentin Creuzet, available on request through E162's type foundry.
description: Designed by Quentin Creuzet and released in 2018 under the E162 foundry, IJBurg is a grotesque sans-serif covering 507 characters. The typeface is distributed on a bespoke basis rather than through standard retail channels, with specimens available via print.e162.eu. Its restrained character suits both editorial text settings and display use.
foundry_blurb: E162 present IJBurg as a typeface by Quentin Creuzet, available on request, with a printed specimen available through their dedicated print site. The family covers 507 characters and is distributed directly by the designer.

name: Galmi
classification_notes: Galmi presents in two styles — Normal and Noir — with no explicit geometric or humanist references, placing it in the broad grotesque tradition typical of French independent type design.
summary: A two-weight grotesque by Léo Guibert, released through the French independent foundry E162 in 2017.
description: Designed by Léo Guibert and available in Normal and Noir weights, Galmi is a compact offering from E162 with a character set of 434 glyphs. The foundry distributes it on request, with specimen printing available via a dedicated print site. Its limited weight range gives it a focused, purposeful character suited to editorial and print contexts.
foundry_blurb: E162 present Galmi as a typeface by Léo Guibert, available in Normal and Noir styles, with typographies available on request and physical specimens accessible through their print site.
